title: Smart QR marketing landing page (single page) with contact form + embedded Login/Register

requirements.summary: Implement a single-page Smart QR marketing landing page with Product Overview, Feature Highlights, Contact, and an embedded Login/Register view loaded from a configurable URL.
acceptanceCriteria: Landing page renders as the primary app route and is usable on desktop and mobile.
Page contains clearly labeled sections for Product Overview, Feature Highlights, Contact, and an embedded Login/Register area.
All user-facing text is in English.
Embed loads inside the page via a configurable URL (e.g., environment/config value) so it can be pointed at the existing Smart QR Login/Register deployment without code changes.

file_operations.description: Create the primary app route as a single-page landing experience that renders the required sections (Product Overview, Feature Highlights, Contact, embedded Login/Register) and provides responsive layout. Use shadcn-ui components (e.g., Card/Button/Input/Textarea as available under frontend/src/components/ui) to build consistent section structure and form shell. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Add an embedded Login/Register area component that renders an iframe (or equivalent embed container) whose src is read from a configurable value (env/config). Include basic UX for missing/invalid URL (e.g., explanatory message) while keeping all text in English.

Add a small frontend config helper that exposes a configurable embed URL (e.g., from Vite env like VITE_SMART_QR_EMBED_URL / similar) for the embedded Login/Register view, without requiring code changes to repoint deployments.
Ensure the document title and metadata remain appropriate for a marketing landing page (English text) and align with the Smart QR branding used in the landing page UI.

requirements.summary: Build the contact form UX with Name/Email/Message fields, client-side validation, and loading/success/error submission states.
acceptanceCriteria: Form includes fields: Name, Email, Message; all required; Email must be valid format.
On submit, the form shows a loading state and prevents duplicate submissions.
On success, show a clear confirmation message and reset the form.
On failure, show a clear error message without losing the user’s typed inputs (except as appropriate).

file_operations.description: Implement the contact form UI with controlled inputs for Name/Email/Message, client-side validation (required fields + email format), and visible submission states (loading disables submit, success confirmation, error message). Use shadcn-ui form-friendly components (e.g., Input/Textarea/Button/Card/Alert as available) to keep styling consistent. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Add a landing-page section wrapper for the contact area that provides section heading/description (English) and renders ContactForm with appropriate spacing and responsive layout. Use shadcn-ui layout primitives where appropriate (e.g., Card). Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Wire contact form submission to the Motoko backend using existing actor + React Query patterns without modifying immutable hook/UI source files.
acceptanceCriteria: Submitting the contact form calls the Motoko backend method and stores the submission.
Frontend uses React Query mutation patterns for submission and invalidation/refetch where appropriate.

No changes are made to files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts(.tsx), frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui source files (compose them only).

file_operations.description: Create a React Query mutation hook that uses useActor() to call the backend capability for contact submission (backendInterface.submitContactForm). Package Name/Email/Message into the payload as needed while backend support evolves (e.g., serialize as a single string) and surface mutation states for the UI. Do not modify immutable hook files.

Wire the form submit handler to useSubmitContactForm() and implement duplicate-submit prevention via mutation loading state; on success reset inputs and show confirmation; on error preserve typed values and show an error message. Use shadcn-ui components for alerts/buttons as available. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Ensure the landing page composes the Contact section that uses the wired ContactForm, and that no changes are required in immutable files (useActor/useInternetIdentity/main.tsx/components/ui sources).

requirements.summary: Apply a coherent, responsive visual theme across the landing page with a non-blue/non-purple primary palette.
acceptanceCriteria: Landing page uses a consistent design system (type scale, spacing, button/input styles) across all sections.
Primary palette is not blue/purple; use a distinct alternative (e.g., charcoal + emerald + warm neutrals) consistently.
Design is responsive and visually polished (no overlapping elements, readable contrast, consistent alignment).

file_operations.description: Define global theme tokens (CSS variables used by Tailwind/shadcn) and base typography/spacing defaults for the app. Set primary colors to a non-blue/non-purple palette (e.g., charcoal + emerald + warm neutrals) and ensure readable contrast in light/dark as applicable.

Adjust Tailwind theme extensions only as needed to support the chosen non-blue/non-purple palette and consistent typography/spacing on the landing page (prefer using existing CSS variable-driven shadcn theme tokens where possible).

Apply consistent section spacing, typography scale, and component styling across Product Overview, Feature Highlights, Contact, and embedded Login/Register area using Tailwind utility classes and composed shadcn-ui components. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create a responsive Product Overview section component with English copy, consistent typography, and clear layout. Use shadcn-ui components (e.g., Card) where appropriate to match the global theme. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create a responsive Feature Highlights section component (e.g., grid of highlights) with English copy and consistent styling that matches the non-blue/non-purple theme. Use shadcn-ui components (e.g., Card/Badge) where available. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Compose ProductOverviewSection and FeatureHighlightsSection into the landing page in the correct order with clear section labels/headings and responsive behavior (mobile-first).
